authors_affiliation: Ran Duan, Xiaodong He, Zhuoran Ouyang; Edan Diagnostics ltd., Shenzhen, China

result.evaluation_split: official hidden full test set (score 0.236, rank 18th of 41); a separate 'validation score' of 0.446 was also officially reported on a validation split

self_reported_metrics.other: Official validation score of 0.446 (challenge weighted-accuracy metric) on the challenge validation split, prior to the 0.236 full hidden test set score

model.architecture_details: MADNN: a 1-dimensional ResNeXt-style stem followed by four stacked 'multi-scale attention modules' that sequentially combine a branch-wise (SKNet-style) attention module and a kernel-wise (SENet-style squeeze-and-excitation) attention module, then global average pooling and a fully-connected output layer. Kernel size in the stem was reduced to 3 (from 7 in standard ResNeXt) to preserve high-frequency ECG features.
model.key_novelty: Combines kernel-wise (SE) and branch-wise (SK) attention sequentially into a single 'multi-scale attention module' on a 1D ResNeXt backbone, allowing each attention type to be optimized separately with diverse attention weights across convolutional kernels.

input.signal_length_window: 60 seconds fixed length (30000 sample points at 500 Hz); shorter signals end-to-end repeated, longer signals truncated from the end; random cropping augmentation applied within 60-sample-point start/end intervals
input.sampling_rate: 500 Hz (resampled via FFT from original 500 Hz or 1000 Hz recordings)
input.preprocessing: FFT-based resampling to 500 Hz, padding/truncation to a fixed 60-second (30000 sample point) length, random cropping augmentation during training

data.training_datasets: 43134 samples obtained from the PhysioNet/Computing in Cardiology Challenge 2020 official training data; no external data reported; 27 scored diagnosis classes used; data split 80% train / 10% validation / 10% test
data.class_imbalance_handling: Focal loss as the training loss; additionally a per-class balance factor (Wi = 2*mu_i/mu) used to probabilistically skip/downsample over-represented classes during training
data.augmentation_techniques: Random cropping of the padded signal within reserved 60-sample-point start and end intervals

training.loss_function: Binary cross-entropy per label (paper also states focal loss was adopted to address class imbalance)
training.optimizer: Adam (initial learning rate 0.0001, beta1=0.9, beta2=0.999, epsilon=1e-08), with learning rate reduced by factor 0.5 after 5 epochs without validation loss improvement
training.cross_validation_strategy: not reported (single 80/10/10 train/validation/test split; a separate ensemble classifier was evaluated via cross-validation on the local testing set)

contribution.summary: Proposes MADNN, a 1D ResNeXt-based network with a novel multi-scale attention module that sequentially combines SENet-style kernel-wise attention and SKNet-style branch-wise attention, applied to 12-lead ECG arrhythmia classification.
